entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop (Pennisetum glaucum) studied as an alternate host for ergot pathogens.

entities.name: CLAVICEPS AFRICANA
entities.description: A fungal pathogen that causes ergot disease in sorghum and can infect pearl millet.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The region where the infectivity of C. africana on pearl millet was studied.

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop that is a primary host for C. africana.

entities.name: ZIMBABWE
entities.description: The country where experiments on C. africana and male-sterile sorghum were conducted.

entities.name: CLAVICEPS FUSIFORMIS
entities.description: A fungal pathogen that establishes disease on pearl millet lines.

entities.name: ICMSR 260
entities.description: A specific genotype of pearl millet that showed high disease incidence.

entities.name: DISEASE INCIDENCE
entities.description: The proportion of plants showing symptoms of disease.

entities.name: DISEASE SEVERITY
entities.description: The intensity or extent of disease symptoms on the host.

entities.name: SPHACELIAL STAGE
entities.description: A specific stage of fungal infection characterized by conidial characteristics.

entities.name: INFECTIVITY
entities.description: The capacity of a pathogen to cause infection in a host.
